item.text: The local skill file has a specific visual-evidence rule; I’m reading that section now and will answer in those terms.

item.aggregatedOutput:
- `What changed`
- `Why it matters`
- `Review focus`
- `Screenshots`
- `Validation`

Cover the useful story:

- what changed
- why it changed
- user or developer impact
- root cause when the PR is a fix
- checks used to validate it

Keep validation calm and evidentiary. Prefer `Passed: scripts/validate test
(468 feature tests)` over a giant standalone `TEST SUCCEEDED` line.

For UI, frontend, visual, or design-facing changes, include screenshots or short
clips in the PR body. Place images near the explanation they support, with
captions that tell the reviewer what to notice. If real captures are not
available yet, keep the PR draft and say exactly what screenshots are still
needed before it is ready for review.

   457	## User preferences
   458	
   459	- when simplifying skill frameworks, the user said "our version will still be bigger because we added sections" and then "keep the additional content we added though ! you removed it all !" -> simplify the top-level contract, but preserve deeper calibration/reference content in secondary files [Task 1]
   460	- on audit/report output, the user wanted `.agents/outputs/` or standard CWD conventions instead of custom destinations -> default saved outputs to repo/CWD conventions before inventing a new folder [Task 1]
   461	- the user said the Drafts plugin felt "overly robotic" -> default Drafts-facing UX to editorial, writing-first language and lightweight onboarding, not visible machinery [Task 1]
   462	- when asking "Is my explain skill implicitly invokable? Should it be? Token cost?" the user wanted a direct state + judgment + cost answer, not a vague discussion [Task 2]
   463	- when the user said "Evalute my Refactor skill ... and I also want to rename it. No changes yet." -> review first, then edit only after explicit approval; keep requested fields like `Hard-cut` unchanged [Task 2]
   464	- for Ultra-Review, the user said "My Ultra review skills should launch three sub-agents in this thread" -> preserve three-subagent standard review as the expected behavior and verify the runtime path, not just the prose [Task 3]
   465	- for Ideate, the user approved "Subject Discovery" and asked for a "separate reference file" -> keep the main skill lean and move specialized runtime behavior into a referenced file [Task 3]
   466	
   467	## Reusable knowledge
   468	
   469	- `ux-audit` works best with simple top-level modes while `design-audit-framework.md` holds the deeper product-type calibration, generated-design fidelity, craft heuristics, and fuller report structure [Task 1]
   470	- Drafts plugin structure after cleanup is `drafts` front door, `compose` for drafting/revision, and `writing-voice` for style setup; `writing-review` was removed, and the visible style guide should focus on observable writing choices rather than stylometry machinery [Task 1]
   471	- `plugins/dots/skills/explain/agents/openai.yaml` controls implicit invocation via `allow_implicit_invocation`; routing cost questions should separate always-loaded metadata from full `SKILL.md` body size [Task 2]
   472	- `plugins/meta-skill/scripts/metaskill validate <skill-dir> --json` and `scripts/verify.sh` are the expected validation spine after skill edits [Task 2][Task 3]
   473	- For runtime fan-out bugs, inspect actual tool exposure with `tool_search`; `multi_agent_v1.spawn_agent` existed, so Ultra-Review needed discovery-before-fallback wording rather than a bigger architectural change [Task 3]
   474	- The Ideate `Subject Discovery` path should read as an entry path back into normal Ideate: require some substance, allow one bounded read-only grounding pass when repo-grounded, return 3-5 candidate subjects, recommend one, then end with one A/B/C/D question [Task 3]
   475	
   476	## Failures and how to do differently
   477	
   478	- The first `ux-audit` simplification deleted too much value; move depth into references instead of flattening it away [Task 1]
   479	- Remaining Drafts risk is behavioral, not structural; dogfood with representative prompts before adding more visible framework [Task 1]
   480	- Trigger overlap is a real repo maintenance pressure, especially `explain` vs `docs-writer` vs `html-artifact`, `product-design` vs `visual-design` vs `design-critique` vs `ux-audit`, and `research` vs `oracle` [Task 2]
   481	- When a skill seems to skip its promised subagent fan-out, check actual tool discovery first. A previous run incorrectly claimed subagents were unavailable without searching for the tool [Task 3]
   482	- Dirty Dots worktrees are common; stage only the intended skill files before commit, then run `scripts/sync-plugins.sh` if the branch is `main` [Task 3]

item.text:
I’d update the PR body’s `Screenshots` section so the screen list is no longer text-only. Each listed screen should have an embedded screenshot or short clip plus a caption explaining what the reviewer should notice.

Example shape:

```md
## Screenshots

### Settings screen
![Settings screen after layout update](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/...)
Caption: Shows the updated spacing, control alignment, and final empty/error state behavior.

### Detail screen
![Detail screen updated visual treatment](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/...)
Caption: Shows the revised hierarchy and responsive layout at the changed breakpoint.
```

If the real captures are not available yet, I would keep the PR as draft and update the body with an explicit blocker like: `Still needed before ready for review: screenshots for Settings screen, Detail screen, and mobile breakpoint.` I would not close out with only the text list.
